metadata.title: retinal_sim validation audit report
scene_input_assumptions: RGB is interpreted as a display-referred color that is reconstructed into a Smits-inspired D65-optimized reflectance spectrum.
The recovered spectrum is inferred from RGB and is not a unique physical scene spectrum.
This mode models diffuse reflectance under D65 for comparative PoC work.

retinal_physiology.scope_note: This simulation stops at the photoreceptor/retinal front end. It does not include post-receptoral, retinal-circuit, or cortical perception modeling.

lambda_max_provenance.source: Human lambda-max anchors follow the project architecture/spec reference set and are used with the unchanged Govardovskii A1 nomogram.
lambda_max_provenance.notes: Fixed species-reference peak wavelengths; not individualized or adaptation-dependent in-vivo fits.

density_function_provenance.source: Human density falloff is a proof-of-concept Gaussian approximation referencing Curcio 1990-style foveal/rod-free-zone anatomy.
density_function_provenance.notes: The current model is radially symmetric and patch-limited; it is not a full histological density map.

naka_rushton_provenance.source: Human Naka-Rushton parameters are configurable retinal-front-end model parameters stored in YAML.
naka_rushton_provenance.notes: Defaults preserve prior proof-of-concept behavior and should not be over-read as validated species-specific transduction measurements.

aperture_weighting.notes: Optional retinal-front-end refinement; many human cone apertures remain sub-pixel at the current proof-of-concept grid scale.

visual_streak.notes: Human foveal modeling stays radially symmetric in this proof-of-concept build.

warnings: Visual-inspection checks remain labeled as visual/manual evidence, not strict quantitative proof.
Several validation checks use proof-of-concept simplifications documented in SCRATCHPAD.md.
Retinal physiology settings here remain front-end assumptions only; no post-receptoral or cortical model is implied.
claim_calibration_notes: A passing report supports only the implemented checks and their declared claim scopes.
Internal consistency checks and external empirical alignment are reported separately and should not be conflated.
R3 scene-input semantics remain active: RGB-inferred scene spectra are not unique measured spectra unless the input mode says otherwise.
R4 retinal physiology provenance remains front-end-only evidence and does not imply full physiological or perceptual validation.

architecture_coverage.architecture_ref: Architecture §11e Retinal image scaling across species

architecture_coverage.note: Counts stimulated receptors inside the projected object footprint.
Uses a fixed deterministic RGB-inferred reflectance regression dataset under D65.
Uses the same D65 observer model as the reflectance-under-D65 basis construction for reprojection.
Uses deterministic RGB probes to sanity-check cone-catch ordering under the declared RGB-inferred D65 reconstruction model.
Measures empirical sinusoidal contrast transfer against the implemented PSF MTF.

architecture_coverage.architecture_ref: Architecture §2a Pupil throughput and aperture geometry
architecture_coverage.note: Report-layer pupil-area diagnostic normalised to the human reference pupil.

architecture_coverage.architecture_ref: Architecture §2b Slit pupil anisotropic blur diagnostics
architecture_coverage.note: Report-layer elliptical Gaussian point-response diagnostic for the cat slit pupil.
Checks signed LCA offsets and minimum blur near the 555 nm reference focus.
Samples species media transmission tables and visualizes delivered spectra.
Uses fixed confusion/control stimulus matrices with median-based comparisons.

architecture_coverage.architecture_ref: Architecture §11c Cone density to sampling limit
architecture_coverage.note: Converts local cone density to cycles/degree using focal-length magnification.
Compares measured 1 mm² counts against density-model expectations.
Quantifies center-vs-periphery contrast loss on a checkerboard stimulus.

dog.source: Miller & Murphy 1995, approx 20/75; arch doc range 4-8 arcmin

cat.source: Blake et al. 1974, 20/100-20/200; MAR 5-10 arcmin; arch doc range 6-10 arcmin

human.density_basis: Human foveal cone packing from the project density model, compared against architecture target values.
human.source: retinal_sim_architecture.md §11c; project density-model expectation for human fovea.
human.claim_note: Reference band represents literature-aligned sampling expectations, not a direct behavioral acuity measurement.

dog.density_basis: Dog area-centralis cone density interpreted through the hexagonal spacing approximation.
dog.claim_note: Used as an externally grounded range for density-derived retinal sampling limits.

cat.density_basis: Cat area-centralis cone density interpreted through the hexagonal spacing approximation.
cat.claim_note: Reference band is approximate and should not be overread as full perceptual validation.

expected_optical_consequences: Smallest reference pupil area in the default species configs.
Largest retinal image magnification among the three modeled species.
human.source: Species optical config plus R1/R2 reporting assumptions.

expected_optical_consequences: Higher throughput than human under the default pupil geometry.
Smaller retinal image magnification than human because focal length is shorter.
dog.source: Species optical config plus R1/R2 reporting assumptions.

expected_optical_consequences: Throughput exceeds human under the default slit approximation.
Axis-dependent blur is expected when slit anisotropy is active.
Retinal image magnification falls between dog and human from focal length alone.
cat.source: Species optical config plus slit-pupil approximation used by the current optical stage.

optical_geometry_expectations.claim_note: These rows are reference expectations derived from modeled pupil geometry and focal length, not direct in-vivo measurements from the simulator.

human.expected_pattern: Blue attenuation stronger than red transmission.
human.uv_shortwave_transmission: most attenuated of the three species in the local reference tables
human.source: Local ocular-media CSV reference used by retinal_sim/optical/media.py
human.claim_note: Assumption table reflects the configured reference transmission curve, not patient-specific ocular media.

dog.expected_pattern: Blue attenuation present, but less severe than the human reference curve.
dog.uv_shortwave_transmission: higher shortwave transmission than the human reference curve
dog.source: Local ocular-media CSV reference used by retinal_sim/optical/media.py
dog.claim_note: External alignment is to the configured species reference curve, not to a full age- or state-dependent physiological model.

cat.expected_pattern: Blue attenuation present, with shortwave transmission distinct from both human and dog reference curves.
cat.uv_shortwave_transmission: higher shortwave transmission than the human reference curve
cat.source: Local ocular-media CSV reference used by retinal_sim/optical/media.py
cat.claim_note: Reference evidence documents configured assumptions about delivered spectra through the ocular media.

wavelength_transmission_assumptions.global_note: These are report-layer reference assumptions used to calibrate claims about wavelength-dependent delivery to the retina.

summary: PASSED: 19/19 implemented validation checks passed for their stated scopes and assumptions

results.inputs_summary: Three analytic scene width/distance cases using the human optical model.
assumptions: Exact 2*arctan geometry is the source of truth.
Human optical focal length is used only to build SceneDescription.
results.method: Compare computed angular width against the closed-form geometric equation.
results.pass_criterion: Absolute angular error < 0.01 degrees for every case.
results.external_reference_summary: Closed-form geometry check with no external literature table required.
results.evidence_basis: Closed-form or invariant check against an analytic expectation.
results.claim_scope_note: Supports scene-geometry arithmetic only; it does not validate downstream retinal or perceptual behavior.
limitations: Checks representative cases, not exhaustive parameter sweeps.
artifacts: Bar chart: expected vs computed angular width

results.pass_criterion: Species ratios agree within 1% relative error.

results.architecture_ref: Architecture §0 Accommodation demand and residual defocus
results.inputs_summary: Near-view scene at 0.25 m for human, dog, and cat.
assumptions: Accommodation is a hard cutoff.
Max accommodation values are fixed PoC constants.
results.method: Compare computed residual defocus against max(0, demand - max_accommodation).
results.pass_criterion: Residual defocus matches expected value within 0.1 diopters.
results.external_reference_summary: Closed-form hard-cutoff accommodation check with no separate external evidence table.
results.evidence_basis: Closed-form or invariant check against an analytic expectation.
results.claim_scope_note: Supports the hard-cutoff accommodation rule currently implemented, not a full accommodation physiology model.
limitations: No lead/lag accommodation response model is applied.
artifacts: Expected vs computed residual defocus bars

results.inputs_summary: Constant-size grayscale object that remains smaller than the 2° patch across all tested distances.
assumptions: Stimulated receptors are those whose mapped positions fall inside the projected object footprint.
results.method: Measure stimulated receptor counts at multiple viewing distances and compare against ideal inverse-square scaling.
results.pass_criterion: Far/near stimulated-receptor scaling stays within 40% relative error of ideal 1/d^2.
results.external_reference_summary: Checks inverse-square geometric consistency inside the current pipeline rather than direct external behavioral data.
results.evidence_basis: Internal regression or consistency check within the simulator's declared implementation and assumptions.
results.claim_scope_note: Supports geometric scaling behavior under current pipeline assumptions only.
limitations: Density gradients and stochastic mosaic sampling still introduce some deviation from exact inverse-square behaviour.
artifacts: Distance vs stimulated receptor count plot
Log-log ideal vs measured scaling plot

results.inputs_summary: Fixed near-metamer RGB pair dataset interpreted as RGB-inferred reflectance under D65 and integrated against human receptor sensitivities.
assumptions: Dataset is repo-local and deterministic to keep the audit reproducible.
The spectra are inferred from RGB with the Smits-inspired D65-optimized reflectance model rather than measured directly.
results.method: Upsample each RGB pair in reflectance_under_d65 mode, integrate against human sensitivities, and bound the maximum cone-catch relative difference.
results.pass_criterion: Maximum cone-catch relative difference < 3.5% for every dataset pair.
results.external_reference_summary: Compared against a compact deterministic reference panel intended to approximate external metamer behavior under the declared scene assumptions.
results.evidence_basis: Comparison against a local deterministic reference table representing published or externally grounded expectations.
results.claim_scope_note: Supports a compact external-style regression panel under D65 assumptions only.
limitations: Dataset is compact rather than exhaustive; it is intended as a deterministic regression panel.
This is an RGB-inferred regression check, not evidence of uniquely recovered physical spectra.

results.inputs_summary: Eight canonical sRGB colors passed through RGB-inferred reflectance reconstruction and D65-based reprojection.
assumptions: The same D65-referenced observer model used to build the basis spectra is used for reprojection.
This checks the declared reflectance_under_d65 inference path, not measured scene spectra.
results.method: Project RGB-inferred reflectance spectra back to sRGB and compute per-colour RMSE.
results.pass_criterion: RMSE < 2 8-bit counts for all tested colours.
results.external_reference_summary: Internal regression check for the declared RGB-inference path under D65 assumptions.
results.evidence_basis: Internal regression or consistency check within the simulator's declared implementation and assumptions.
results.claim_scope_note: Supports the current RGB-inferred reflectance reconstruction path, not unique recovery of scene spectra.
limitations: This validates the D65 round-trip path, not arbitrary illuminant changes.

results.inputs_summary: Deterministic RGB probe panel spanning violet-to-red anchors plus mixed colors, interpreted as RGB-inferred reflectance and integrated with D65-weighted species sensitivities.
assumptions: The panel is RGB-defined and deterministic; it is a regression sanity check rather than a physical monochromator dataset.
Spectra are inferred from RGB using the reflectance_under_d65 model.
results.method: Upsample each probe color in reflectance_under_d65 mode, compute D65-weighted cone catches for each species, and verify expected dominance/order and blue-vs-warm separations.
results.pass_criterion: Anchor colors preserve expected cone-type dominance and dog/cat blue-vs-yellow/red separations exceed the fixed margin.
results.external_reference_summary: Internal response-order regression for the current D65-weighted RGB inference model.
results.evidence_basis: Internal regression or consistency check within the simulator's declared implementation and assumptions.
results.claim_scope_note: Supports ordering behavior in the declared RGB-inference model, not full spectral physiology.
limitations: Because the probes originate from sRGB values, this validates response ordering under the current D65-based reconstruction model rather than true narrowband physiology.

results.expected: Measured sinusoidal contrast transfer matches predicted MTF within 0.081
results.details:
5 cpd: measured=0.9897, predicted=0.9897, |Δ|=0.0000 ✓
10 cpd: measured=0.9597, predicted=0.9597, |Δ|=0.0000 ✓
20 cpd: measured=0.8495, predicted=0.8495, |Δ|=0.0000 ✓
30 cpd: measured=0.7787, predicted=0.6983, |Δ|=0.0804 ✓
results.inputs_summary: Human 550 nm PSF tested with sinusoidal gratings spanning low-to-mid spatial frequencies.
assumptions: The implemented PSF is a diffraction-derived Gaussian approximation.
results.method: Measure empirical contrast transfer on sinusoidal gratings and compare it with the PSF-predicted MTF.
results.pass_criterion: Measured modulation stays within 0.08 of the predicted MTF at all tested spatial frequencies.
results.external_reference_summary: Compares measured modulation against the simulator's own implemented diffraction-derived PSF model.
results.evidence_basis: Internal regression or consistency check within the simulator's declared implementation and assumptions.
results.claim_scope_note: Supports the implemented optical approximation, not blanket validation of all anterior-eye optics.
limitations: Validation targets the implemented diffraction-derived PSF model, not a full Airy-pattern optical stack.

results.inputs_summary: Gaussian PSF kernels across the canonical wavelength grid for all three species.
assumptions: Kernel normalization in float64 is the source of truth for this check.
results.method: Sum each PSF kernel and measure max absolute deviation from 1.0.
results.external_reference_summary: Kernel normalization is an analytic invariant rather than an externally benchmarked physiological claim.
results.evidence_basis: Closed-form or invariant check against an analytic expectation.
results.claim_scope_note: Supports numerical normalization of the PSF kernels only.
limitations: Only validates discrete kernel normalization, not broader optical energy accounting.
artifacts: Log-scale wavelength vs normalization error plot

results.inputs_summary: Human, dog, and cat species configs with circular/slit pupil geometries.
assumptions: Pupil area is the relevant throughput proxy for the validation report.
Cat slit height is read from the species optical config when present.
results.method: Compute pupil area for each species, normalize to the human reference pupil, and compare the resulting throughput ratios.
results.pass_criterion: Species pupil areas differ and human-normalized throughput increases from human to dog/cat.

results.architecture_ref: Architecture §2b Slit pupil anisotropic blur diagnostics
results.inputs_summary: Cat slit pupil evaluated with a point-response diagnostic at 550 nm.
assumptions: The diagnostic uses an elliptical Gaussian approximation for the slit pupil.
Horizontal blur should exceed vertical blur for the vertical slit approximation.
results.method: Generate a point-spread diagnostic, measure axis-specific sigma/FWHM, and require horizontal spread to exceed vertical spread.
results.pass_criterion: Cat diagnostic reports sigma_x > sigma_y and FWHM_x > FWHM_y while remaining normalized.
results.external_reference_summary: Aligned to the local slit-pupil optical consequence table; evidence remains approximate because the model uses a slit approximation.

results.evidence_basis: Comparison against a local deterministic reference table representing published or externally grounded expectations.
results.claim_scope_note: Supports axis-aware blur reporting in the current slit approximation, not full feline ocular optics.
limitations: This report-layer approximation anticipates the axis-aware optical stage rather than replacing it.

results.expected: Short and long wavelengths carry opposite-signed LCA offsets and the smallest PSF width occurs near 555 nm
results.inputs_summary: Canonical wavelength grid evaluated for all three species using the current Gaussian-plus-LCA optical model.
assumptions: R2 models LCA as a signed wavelength-dependent defocus offset around a 555 nm reference focus.
The smallest blur should occur near the reference wavelength when scene defocus is zero.
results.method: Sample per-wavelength LCA offsets and PSF widths from the optical-stage metadata and verify sign/order behavior.
results.pass_criterion: Offsets are positive in the short wavelengths, negative in the long wavelengths, and minimum PSF width occurs near 555 nm for each species.
results.external_reference_summary: Aligned to local wavelength-delivery and optical-behavior reference assumptions rather than direct in-vivo PSF measurements.

results.inputs_summary: Species ocular-media transmission tables sampled on the canonical wavelength grid and applied to a unit spectrum.
assumptions: R2 uses fixed species reference transmission curves rather than age- or state-dependent media variants.
Short-wavelength attenuation should exceed long-wavelength attenuation for each species.
results.method: Sample the configured transmission curves, compare short-vs-long wavelength attenuation, and visualize the delivered spectra.
results.pass_criterion: Each species transmits less blue than red and the sampled transmission curves differ across species.
results.external_reference_summary: Aligned to the configured species ocular-media reference curves and their expected shortwave-vs-longwave behavior.

results.inputs_summary: Species-specific acuity prediction using Snellen E orientation discriminability.
assumptions: Published acuity ranges are broad PoC acceptance bands.
Letter-region masking and Pearson correlation distance are required.
results.method: Predict minimum resolvable angular size for each species and compare with expected ranges and ordering.
results.pass_criterion: Each species falls within its target range and human acuity is best.
results.external_reference_summary: Aligned to published species acuity target ranges stored locally for deterministic reporting.

external_reference_table.source: Snellen 20/20 standard; MAR = 1 arcmin
Miller & Murphy 1995, approx 20/75; arch doc range 4-8 arcmin
Blake et al. 1974, 20/100-20/200; MAR 5-10 arcmin; arch doc range 6-10 arcmin

results.evidence_basis: Comparison against a local deterministic reference table representing published or externally grounded expectations.
results.claim_scope_note: Supports approximate species acuity alignment at the retinal front end, not full behavioral or cortical vision validation.
limitations: Cat predictions can be seed-sensitive near threshold.

results.inputs_summary: Fixed confusion-pair panels for dog and cat plus a shared control-pair panel.
assumptions: Stimulus scaling is applied to keep Naka-Rushton responses out of saturation.
results.method: Compute species-wise discriminability distributions across deterministic confusion/control stimulus matrices and compare confusion medians against controls.
results.pass_criterion: Each species-specific confusion median is suppressed relative to its controls, and human exceeds cat on the cat confusion set.
results.external_reference_summary: Aligned indirectly to expected species confusion-axis behavior; evidence is illustrative rather than exhaustive.
results.evidence_basis: Comparison against a local deterministic reference table representing published or externally grounded expectations.
results.claim_scope_note: Supports qualitative species confusion-axis differences at the retinal front end, not complete color appearance prediction.
limitations: Compact panel intended for deterministic regression rather than exhaustive colour-space coverage.

results.inputs_summary: Area-centralis cone densities converted to spacing and cycles/degree for all species.
assumptions: Hexagonal spacing approximation is used to map cone density to Nyquist limit.
results.method: Convert local cone density to spacing, then convert spacing to cycles/degree using focal-length-based retinal magnification.
results.pass_criterion: Each species falls inside its target cycles/degree band.
results.external_reference_summary: Aligned to density-derived species Nyquist target ranges stored locally for deterministic reporting.

results.inputs_summary: Generated mosaics compared against density-model expectations over the simulated square patch.
assumptions: Species density functions are the literature-backed PoC source of truth for expected counts.
results.method: Compare measured receptor counts and cone/rod composition against integrated density-model expectations over the simulated patch.
results.pass_criterion: Total count error < 25% and cone/rod composition error < 15% for every species.
results.external_reference_summary: Compares generated mosaics against the project's density-model expectations; evidence remains indirect until tied to richer external histology tables.
results.evidence_basis: Internal regression or consistency check within the simulator's declared implementation and assumptions.
results.claim_scope_note: Supports the current mosaic-density implementation at the retinal front end, not full retinal histology.
limitations: Current model still uses simplified analytic density functions rather than digitised histology maps.

results.inputs_summary: Red-green split stimulus simulated for human, dog, and cat.
assumptions: Left/right response norm is used as the summary metric.
results.method: Measure left/right cone-response difference after full-pipeline simulation.
results.pass_criterion: Human left/right difference exceeds dog by at least 20%.
results.external_reference_summary: Proxy end-to-end comparison that illustrates retained information differences without claiming perceptual equivalence.
results.evidence_basis: Internal regression or consistency check within the simulator's declared implementation and assumptions.
results.claim_scope_note: Illustrates comparative retinal-information loss and should not be read as direct conscious appearance.
limitations: This is a compact proxy for a richer end-to-end color-deficit comparison.

results.inputs_summary: Checkerboard stimulus simulated for human, dog, and cat.
assumptions: Checkerboard bright/dark contrast is used as the resolvability proxy.
results.method: Compare center vs peripheral bright/dark response contrast on a fine checkerboard and compare human center contrast against dichromat center contrast.
results.pass_criterion: Human center contrast exceeds human periphery and exceeds dog/cat center contrast.
results.external_reference_summary: Proxy end-to-end contrast-retention check, not a full empirical eccentricity validation.
results.evidence_basis: Internal regression or consistency check within the simulator's declared implementation and assumptions.
results.claim_scope_note: Illustrates front-end contrast retention trends and should not be read as a full perceptual validation.
limitations: Quantifies contrast retention on a fine checkerboard but does not yet fit a full eccentricity-dependent MTF curve.
